<title level="1" right="Sorcerer Bloodline " pfs="Standard">[Aberrant](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=1)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 195

Something speaks to you from beyond the stars or below the earth. Ancient and unknowable, this alien influence presses against your mind.

<column gap="tiny">

**Spell List**
[Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Bloodline Skills**
[Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7),
[Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61)
- **1st** [Spider Sting](/Spells.aspx?ID=300)
- **2nd** [Touch of Idiocy](/Spells.aspx?ID=341)
- **3rd** [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354)
- **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48)
- **5th** [Black Tentacles](/Spells.aspx?ID=23)
- **6th** [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112)
- **7th** [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369)
- **8th** [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=347)
- **9th** [Unfathomable Song](/Spells.aspx?ID=349)

**Bloodline Spells**
- **Initial** [Tentacular Limbs](/Spells.aspx?ID=517)
- **Advanced** [Aberrant Whispers](/Spells.aspx?ID=491)
- **Greater** [Unusual Anatomy](/Spells.aspx?ID=519)

**Blood Magic**
Aberrant whispers shield one target’s mind or your own, granting a +2 status bonus to Will saving throws for 1 round.

</column>

<title level="1" right="Sorcerer Bloodline " pfs="Standard">[Undead](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=10)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 198

The touch of undeath runs through your blood. Your family tree might contain powerful undead, like a vampire, or perhaps you died and returned a bit different.

<column gap="tiny">

**Spell List**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

**Bloodline Skills**
[Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7),
[Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35)
- **1st** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146)
- **2nd** [False Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=108)
- **3rd** [Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=22)
- **4th** [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=329)
- **5th** [Cloudkill](/Spells.aspx?ID=42)
- **6th** [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=353)
- **7th** [Finger of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=116)
- **8th** [Horrid Wilting](/Spells.aspx?ID=152)
- **9th** [Wail of the Banshee](/Spells.aspx?ID=361)

**Bloodline Spells**
- **Initial** [Undeath's Blessing](/Spells.aspx?ID=518)
- **Advanced** [Drain Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=502)
- **Greater** [Grasping Grave](/Spells.aspx?ID=512)

**Blood Magic**
Necromantic energy flows through you or one target. Either you gain temporary Hit Points equal to the spell’s level for 1 round, or a target takes 1 negative damage per spell level (if the spell already deals initial negative damage, combine this with the spell’s initial damage before determining weaknesses and resistances).

</column>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 138 Through lineage, magic, or wishes made real, the blood of a noble genie flows through your veins. **Spell List** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) **Bloodline Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) **Granted Spells** - **Cantrip** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) - **1st** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) - **2nd** variable - **3rd** [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104) - **4th** [Creation](/Spells.aspx?ID=54) - **5th** variable - **6th** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) - **7th** [Energy Aegis](/Spells.aspx?ID=100) - **8th** variable - **9th** [Resplendent Mansion](/Spells.aspx?ID=257) **Bloodline Spells** - **Initial** [Genie's Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=783) - **Advanced** [Heart's Desire](/Spells.aspx?ID=784) - **Greater** [Wish-Twisted Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=790) **Blood Magic** Your spellcasting warps reality and distracts your foes. Either you gain a +1 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks for 1 round, or a target takes a –1 status penalty to Perception for 1 round. Genie Type At 1st level, choose the type of genie that influenced your bloodline: [janni](/Monsters.aspx?ID=211), [djinni](/Monsters.aspx?ID=212), [efreeti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=214), [marid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=215), or [shaitan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=213). You can't change your genie type later. This determines what granted spells you receive at 2nd, 5th, and 8th level.
  • **Janni** 2nd: [_create food_](/Spells.aspx?ID=52); 5th: [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=19); 8th: [_scintillating pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=267)
  • **Djinni** 2nd: [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164); 5th: [_illusory scene_](/Spells.aspx?ID=161); 8th: [_punishing winds_](/Spells.aspx?ID=240)
  • **Efreeti** 2nd: [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=102); 5th: [_elemental form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=98) (fire only); 8th: [_maze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=187)
  • **Marid** 2nd: [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371); 5th: [_control water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=51); 8th: [_horrid wilting_](/Spells.aspx?ID=152)
  • **Shaitan** 2nd: [_glitterdust_](/Spells.aspx?ID=136); 5th: [_wall of stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=365); 8th: [_earthquake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=95)

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 138

One of your ancestors was inspired by a [nymph](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=77), or perhaps was a nymph themself, and now the beauty of nature lives in you.

<column gap="tiny">

**Spell List**

<title level="1" right="Sorcerer Bloodline " pfs="Standard">[Psychopomp](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=13)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 138

For good or ill, your ancestors' deeds drew the attention of [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83), or you might somehow count one in your family tree. The presence of these shepherds of souls and enemies of undeath has left an indelible mark on you.

<column gap="tiny">

**Spell List**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

**Bloodline Skills**
[Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7),
[Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Disrupt Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=79)
- **1st** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)
- **2nd** [Calm Emotions](/Spells.aspx?ID=31)
- **3rd** [Searing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=269)
- **4th** [Dimensional Anchor](/Spells.aspx?ID=70)
- **5th** [Death Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=64)
- **6th** [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=301)
- **7th** [Finger of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=116)
- **8th** [Spirit Song](/Spells.aspx?ID=303)
- **9th** [Massacre](/Spells.aspx?ID=186)

**Bloodline Spells**
- **Initial** [Sepulchral Mask](/Spells.aspx?ID=786)
- **Advanced** [Spirit Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=788)
- **Greater** [Shepherd of Souls](/Spells.aspx?ID=787)

**Blood Magic**
The border between life and death becomes blurred to you. Either you gain a +2 status bonus to Fortitude saving throws for 1 round, or a target takes 1 damage per spell level. The damage is negative if the target is living or positive if the target is undead. If the spell already deals that type of damage, combine it with the spell's initial damage before determining weaknesses and resistances.

</column>
name: Psychopomp

<title level="1" right="Sorcerer Bloodline " pfs="Limited">[Phoenix](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=15)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 74

You have been blessed by a phoenix, perhaps via some magical interaction with a similarly blessed individual.

 Grant this new sorcerer bloodline to players who finish the Fists of the Ruby Phoenix Adventure Path. Players can then choose this bloodline for any new sorcerer characters they create for future campaigns.

<column gap="tiny">

**Spell List**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Bloodline Skills**
[Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6),
[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66)
- **1st** [Burning Hands](/Spells.aspx?ID=30)

<title level="1" right="Sorcerer Bloodline " pfs="Standard">[Wyrmblessed](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=17)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 75

You lay claim to the might of dragons, but your powers are sacred instead of arcane—born from a worship of draconic might so powerful it infused your blood or, perhaps, from a celestial or draconic power blessing one of your ancestors received.

<column gap="tiny">

**Spell List**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

**Bloodline Skills**
[Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7),
[Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246)
- **1st** [Mage Armor](/Spells.aspx?ID=176)
- **2nd** [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256)
- **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147)
- **4th** [Reflective Scales](/Spells.aspx?ID=583)
- **5th** [Cloak of Colors](/Spells.aspx?ID=41)
- **6th** [Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=254)
- **7th** [Mask of Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=185)
- **8th** [Divine Inspiration](/Spells.aspx?ID=83)
- **9th** [Overwhelming Presence](/Spells.aspx?ID=212)

**Bloodline Spells**
- **Initial** [Dragon Claws](/Spells.aspx?ID=500)
- **Advanced** [Dragon Breath](/Spells.aspx?ID=499)
- **Greater** [Dragon Wings](/Spells.aspx?ID=501)

**Blood Magic**
Draconic might carries in your voice. Either you gain a +1 status bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks for 1 round, or a target takes a –1 status penalty to Will saves for 1 round.

</column>

<title level="2">Dragon Type</title>

At 1st level, choose the type of dragon that influenced your bloodline. You can't change your dragon type later. This choice affects how some of your bloodline spells function. The good metallic dragons and their damage types are [brass](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=170) (fire), [bronze](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=171) (electricity), [copper](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=172) (acid), [gold](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=173) (fire), and [silver](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=174) (cold). The evil chromatic dragons and their damage types are [black](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=165) (acid), [blue](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=166) (electricity), [green](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=167) (poison), [red](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=168) (fire), and [white](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=169) (cold). The primal dragons of planar origin and their damage types are [brine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=175) (acid), [cloud](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=176) (electricity), [crystal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=177) (piercing), [magma](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=178) (fire), and [umbral](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=179) (negative). The imperial dragons and their damage types are [forest](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=227) (piercing), [sea](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=228) (bludgeoning), [sky](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=229) (electricity), [sovereign](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=230) (mental), and [underworld](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=231) (fire). For the [_dragon breath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=499) focus spell, the area is a 60-foot line for a brine dragon; a 30-foot cone for a cloud, crystal, forest, magma, sovereign, or umbral dragon; and a 10-foot burst within 30 feet for a sea, sky, or underworld dragon.

<title level="2">Related Bloodline</title>

Because the wyrmblessed and [draconic bloodlines](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=5) have similar origins, they count the same as each other for the purposes of prerequisites and access requirements.

You deal 4 mental damage per spell rank (basic Will save) to the first creature that deals damage to you before the end of your next turn; if no creature damages you in that time, you consume that spite to gain temporary Hit Points at the beginning of your next turn equal to the spell's rank. These temporary Hit Points last until the beginning of your following turn.

</column>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 196 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The [_wyrmblessed_](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=17) sorcerer bloodline provides options for primal and imperial dragons. Character with a similar draconic connection, such as draconic bloodline sorcerors or draconic instinct barbarians, can also select from these dragon types. You must own a copy of Lost Omens: The Mwangi Expanse to select these options._ The blood of dragons flows through your veins. These beasts are both fearsome in combat and skilled at magic. **Spell List** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) **Bloodline Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) **Granted Spells** - **Cantrip** [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) - **2nd** [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147) - **4th** [Spell Immunity](/Spells.aspx?ID=296) - **5th** [Chromatic Wall](/Spells.aspx?ID=37) - **6th** [Dragon Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=88) - **7th** [Mask of Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=185) - **8th** [Prismatic Wall](/Spells.aspx?ID=234) - **9th** [Overwhelming Presence](/Spells.aspx?ID=212) **Bloodline Spells** - **Initial** [Dragon Claws](/Spells.aspx?ID=500) - **Advanced** [Dragon Breath](/Spells.aspx?ID=499) - **Greater** [Dragon Wings](/Spells.aspx?ID=501) **Blood Magic** Draconic scales grow briefly on you or one target, granting a +1 status bonus to AC for 1 round. Dragon Type At 1st level, choose the type of dragon that influenced your bloodline. You can't change your dragon type later. This choice affects how some of your bloodline spells function. The good metallic dragons and their damage types are [brass](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=170) (fire), [bronze](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=171) (electricity), [copper](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=172) (acid), [gold](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=173) (fire), and [silver](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=174) (cold). The evil chromatic dragons and their damage types are [black](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=165) (acid), [blue](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=166) (electricity), [green](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=167) (poison), [red](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=168) (fire), and [white](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=169) (cold). Related Bloodlines **Source** [_Mwangi Expanse pg. 76_](https://paizo.com/products/btq027ot)

text: Draconic Source Core Rulebook pg. 196 PFS Note The wyrmblessed sorcerer bloodline provides options for primal and imperial dragons. Character with a similar draconic connection, such as draconic bloodline sorcerors or draconic instinct barbarians, can also select from these dragon types. You must own a copy of Lost Omens: The Mwangi Expanse to select these options. The blood of dragons flows through your veins. These beasts are both fearsome in combat and skilled at magic. Spell List Arcane Bloodline Skills Arcana, Intimidation Granted Spells - Cantrip Shield - 1st True Strike - 2nd Resist Energy - 3rd Haste - 4th Spell Immunity - 5th Chromatic Wall - 6th Dragon Form - 7th Mask of Terror - 8th Prismatic Wall - 9th Overwhelming Presence Bloodline Spells - Initial Dragon Claws - Advanced Dragon Breath - Greater Dragon Wings Blood Magic Draconic scales grow briefly on you or one target, granting a +1 status bonus to AC for 1 round. Dragon Type At 1st level, choose the type of dragon that influenced your bloodline. You can't change your dragon type later. This choice affects how some of your bloodline spells function. The good metallic dragons and their damage types are brass (fire), bronze (electricity), copper (acid), gold (fire), and silver (cold). The evil chromatic dragons and their damage types are black (acid), blue (electricity), green (poison), red (fire), and white (cold). Related Bloodlines Source Mwangi Expanse pg. 76 Because the wyrmblessed and draconic bloodlines have similar origins, they count the same as each other for the purposes of prerequisites and access requirements. <title level="1" right="Sorcerer Bloodline " pfs="Standard">[Elemental](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=6)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 196

A genie ancestor or some other elemental influence has imbued your blood with primal fury, affecting how the bloodline spells and granted spells marked with an asterisk (*), as well as your blood magic, function.

<column gap="tiny">

**Spell List**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Bloodline Skills**
[Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7),
[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236)*
- **1st** [Burning Hands](/Spells.aspx?ID=30)*
- **2nd** [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256)
- **3rd** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119)*
- **4th** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128)
- **5th** [Elemental Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=98)
- **6th** [Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=254)
- **7th** [Energy Aegis](/Spells.aspx?ID=100)
- **8th** [Prismatic Wall](/Spells.aspx?ID=234)
- **9th** [Storm of Vengeance](/Spells.aspx?ID=313)

**Bloodline Spells**
- **Initial** [Elemental Toss](/Spells.aspx?ID=505)*
- **Advanced** [Elemental Motion](/Spells.aspx?ID=504)
- **Greater** [Elemental Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=503)*

**Blood Magic**
Elemental energy surrounds you or a target. Either you gain a +1 status bonus to Intimidation checks for 1 round, or a target takes 1 damage per spell level. The damage is bludgeoning or fire, according to your elemental type (see below). If the spell already deals that type of damage, combine it with the spell’s initial damage before determining weaknesses and resistances.

</column>

<title level="2">Elemental Type</title>

Choose the type of elemental that influenced your bloodline: air, earth, fire, or water. If your element is air, you buffet your foes with powerful winds; if it's earth, you toss huge chunks of rock; if it's fire, you incinerate your foes with flame; and if it's water, you inundate your foes with torrents of water. For fire, all marked spells deal fire damage. For other elements, they deal bludgeoning damage. You replace any existing elemental traits with the trait of the element you chose.
